title: relay.rs creates reqwest::Client::new() twice instead of accepting a shared client
description: relay::add() creates reqwest::Client::new() (line 30) and relay::remove() creates another reqwest::Client::new() (line 167). These are CLI-only code paths so the cost is not a hot path, but the pattern is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase where clients are constructed once and passed in (e.g., delivery::run_worker, feed::poll_all). Client creation initializes a TLS stack and connection pool. The functions should accept &reqwest::Client as a parameter, consistent with the rest of the codebase, and the CLI caller should construct one client and pass it to both. This also makes the functions unit-testable with a mock client.
close_reason: Duplicate of broadside-zzj3.21 (same relay.rs reqwest::Client per-call finding).
